constraints.expression: consumer SHOULD verify pulse_attention_breakdown.strategy + .inter_oracle_conflict + .escalation sums to ≤1.0 (with floating-point tolerance ε=0.01); soft constraint surfaced as ATTENTION_BREAKDOWN_OVERFLOW_SOFT manifest entry when sum > 1.0 by >0.01
constraints.message: pulse_attention_breakdown components SHOULD sum to ≤1.0 within ε=0.01 tolerance. Sum > 1.0 + 0.01 indicates likely producer-side miscalibration; the schema admits the looser shape because some legitimate consumer policies (weighted overlap, priority-stacking) emit overlapping attention.
constraints.evaluation_note: Soft cross-field invariant. The schema enforces the per-component bounds (each in [0, 1]) at the structural layer; the sum-bound is consumer-side because legitimate weighted-overlap policies may exceed 1.0 by design. Runtime-deferred classification surfaces the obligation in the UnverifiedObligationsManifest so consumers asserting strict-sum-bounded policy can reject overlapping attention while consumers with overlap-tolerant policy continue.
